paragraphs.body: Every once in a while, on a boring Sunday afternoon, I decided to clean my bedroom, which I shared with my thirteen-year-old brother. When I decided to clean my room thoroughly, it meant I also cleaned the closet where the dusty, worn-out ballet point shoes that barely fit me were stored. It wasn’t just the pointe shoes that were in the cold, humid, and dust-filled closet; it was my lifelong dream of becoming a professional ballerina dancing in the New York City Ballet. When I encountered the pointe shoes in the dark corner, I reached for them. As I examined them, my chest felt weird. My chest felt warm, as if some part of my body sparked the flame of that dream all over again. Feeling nostalgic, I tried the pointe shoes again, but every time I did that, I felt like one of Cinderella’s sisters because they didn’t fit anymore. I only managed to wear the “box” of the pointe shoes. The rigid box at the front of the pointe shoe is made of layers of fabric, cardboard, and glue, which support the toes and help distribute the weight. I tried to do some steps in the pointe shoes, but as expected, I couldn’t. Not only couldn’t I because more than five years had passed since the last time I wore pointe shoes, but because they also hurt in the front because of how stiff and strong they were. I quickly took them off as I felt the frustration and anger overwhelm me.

annotations.text: it was my lifelong dream of becoming a professional ballerina dancing in the New York City Ballet.

annotations.comment: Nice narrative :) It definitely works as a revealing metaphor. Great opening!
annotations.context_left: flame of that dream all over again. Feeling nostalgic, I tried the pointe shoes again, but every

paragraphs.body: Pointe shoes are the most delicate and feminine shoes a girl can dream of wearing, but they also hurt your feet. They are made of a rigid cast and covered in shiny fabric. Depending on the ballerina’s skin color, the pointe shoes would be the same color as the skin. To secure the pointe shoe to the foot, two ribbons, the same color as the pointe shoe, are sown to the back of the pointe shoes and tied around the ankle. When choosing pointe shoes, you must be very meticulous about the size. It has to be perfect. If it's not the corresponding pointe shoe, you could hurt yourself. The first time trying on the point shoes, this feeling of accomplishment hugs you. As a ballerina, it is a big reward. Dancing in pointe shoes seems painful to people because they don’t understand how it is possible to dance on your tiptoes. When you wear pointe shoes, you put a cushion, made from gel and fabric, on your toes, which helps with the pointe shoe box’s stiffness. Dancing with pointe shoes is scary because all the weight is on the ankles. Although it is difficult to master ballet steps on pointe shoes, it is the most rewarding feeling when you master them. From the public’s perspective, pointe shoes make the ballerina look delicate, elegant, and lightweight. But from the ballerina’s perspective, pointe shoes make the toes feel cramped, create blisters, and your feet are sore because of the arch it creates. It is a sacrifice a ballerina has to make because their feet are destroyed, and after a show, they can bleed, but that is the least of their worries. They are doing what they love alongside the people that they love and with the support of their loved ones.

annotations.text: Pointe shoes are the most delicate and feminine shoes a girl can dream of wearing, but they also hurt your feet.
Dancing with pointe shoes is scary because all the weight is on the ankles.

paragraphs.body: Every day of my life, I regret quitting ballet. Truthfully, I don’t even remember why I quit in the first place. It is frustrating because I know I could’ve gotten far if I pulled it together, but it taught me something valuable in other aspects of my life. Not everything in life is going to be easy; sometimes, I will encounter painful situations, whether physical, mental, or emotional. Those tough moments will sometimes help me get closer to my goals whenever I encounter an obstacle. But I also need to remember those who come along with the process. Those who support you and direct you towards your aspirations. Mostly, those who support you could be your family or people you consider your family, your blood. I had ballet classes every Monday and Wednesday at 5 pm and Saturday mornings at 9. I was in Junior 3, which was the highest category for my age. I had danced 5 years before being allowed to dance in pointe shoes, but I always thought I was in pointe shoes too early because I struggled to advance at the same pace my other friends were. The whole period I danced ballet in pointe shoes, I went on relevé, which is to rise on pointe from flat feet, but the box of the pointe shoe didn’t exactly touch the rubbery, grey floor we danced on as it was supposed to. Other girls made it seem so easy, and what frustrated me the most was that they started at the same time as me. It made me wonder what was wrong with me. As time went by, I started to lose my passion for ballet. Losing that feeling of being inspired made me feel like I was also failing in my mother’s dream. At that time, I didn’t realize I was giving up on my only true passion and hers.

annotations.text: Every day of my life, I regret quitting ballet. Truthfully, I don’t even remember why I quit in the first place. It is frustrating because I know I could’ve gotten far if I pulled it together, but it taught me something valuable in other aspects of my life. Not everything in life is going to be easy; sometimes, I will encounter painful situations, whether physical, mental, or emotional.
annotations.comment: Great job linking the object to the large subject!

paragraphs.body: I thought I was failing my mother. I have always wondered if quitting ballet disappointed her. My mother danced ballet around the same age as me and stopped. When I was in 5th grade, I remember her taking me and my sister to our first ballet class. It was so spontaneous, but little did I know it was my mom introducing us to her passion, too. My sister quit, but I continued dancing ballet for another six years. After those six years, I didn’t think there would be a moment when I told my mother I wasn’t going to dance ballet anymore. As soon as those words came out of my mouth, it made me feel like I had a wound in my heart and I was going to bleed out. It was the moment my dream died.

annotations.text: I thought I was failing my mother. I have always wondered if quitting ballet disappointed her.

paragraphs.body: I don’t remember that moment well. It was really shocking for me, so I blanked the moment after I realized I had quit. I remember how my mom answered peacefully, with the simplest “Okay.” I would never have thought she would let me quit that easily.

annotations.comment: So much honesty and authenticity in your voice!

paragraphs.body: Blood, in this case, didn’t mean a wound was open, just that the doorway of fulfilling my dream closed in the matter of a sentence. I don’t mean real blood. Blood had always scared me. Blood has made me dizzy and sick. Blood means something bad until now. Blood contains the passion that ran through my veins. Blood is the passion that also ran through my mother's veins. Blood is the vital liquid that travels through my veins and arteries. Blood is as vital as my family is to me. Blood is the part of a saying my family has always lived by: "Everything you do, put your blood, sweat, and tears into it.” Blood wasn’t only blood coming out of my wounds and blisters. Blood meant how hard I worked to achieve what I wanted. Blood is how hard my family has worked throughout the years to achieve what they want, to take care of their families, and to pass on their knowledge, effort, and strength. Blood makes me feel connected to my family tree. Their blood is the blood that also runs through my veins. My blood contains the contribution each one of them made to the creation of amazing, hardworking human beings in our family. Blood makes me feel connected to their wisdom, beauty, and culture that has been passed on from generation to generation.

annotations.context_left: your blood, sweat, and tears into it.” Blood wasn’t only blood coming out of my wounds and

paragraphs.body: Although it could sound a little disturbing, I quickly realized that the blood wasn’t only related to my wounds when I danced for hours. It demonstrated how passion for ballet was passed on through generations. My grandma put my mom in ballet classes like my mother introduced her daughters to ballet. My grandmother, my mother, and I share a passion for ballet. My blood shares a passion for ballet. The relationship between my mother and how she is my blood—how her blood is the blood that runs through my veins, the blood that shapes me to be the woman she raised alongside my father. The bloody wounds she helped to heal are the blood that has run through our family history.

I mentioned before that the doorway to fulfilling my dream closed in a matter of a sentence, but I didn’t know then that it was the only doorway I was focusing on. I didn’t look back and noticed how the challenge these pointe shoes gave me allowed me and helped me to open other doors. It taught me the importance of thinking through when deciding and looking for alternatives to achieve my dream. I could still be able to dance in pointe shoes, but at that moment, I got stuck and didn’t think things through. Those pointe shoes in the corner of my dark, humid closet remind me constantly of never shutting the door on that dream, no matter how difficult it gets.

annotations.text: I could still be able to dance in pointe shoes, but at that moment, I got stuck and didn’t think things through. Those pointe shoes in the corner of my dark, humid closet remind me constantly of never shutting the door on that dream, no matter how difficult it gets.
annotations.comment: Nice. Great sense of large subject an purpose. Excellent move.
This last sentence in pink is so strong, I think you could end the essay right here! I don't even think you need the last paragraph at all :)

paragraphs.body: Scary things, like blood, sometimes teach us the deepest meanings in life. The perspective in which we see them will define them. Everything in life has a meaning. It could be as easy as giving up what you love the most because it hurts because it's bleeding or as meaningful as seeing it as the effort you are putting into what you are doing and why you are doing it.

annotations.comment: Fab revision! This is so descriptive and lovely and you're making excellent writing moves. Great sense of purpose. Engaging, authentic voice. A nuanced and sophisticated exploration of family, dreams, and expectations. Well done!!
